section: Chapter 1: Numbers and their Place Value

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the ones place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: What is the smallest natural number?
questions.explanation: Natural numbers start from 1 and continue infinitely.

questions.question: What is the largest digit in a decimal number?
questions.explanation: The maximum value of any digit is 9.

questions.question: What are the first three prime numbers?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers are natural numbers greater than 1 that have no divisors other than 1 and themselves.

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the tens place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: What are the first five even numbers?
questions.explanation: Even numbers are natural numbers divisible by 2.

questions.question: What is the largest prime number less than 100?
questions.explanation: Prime numbers are natural numbers greater than 1 that have no divisors other than 1 and themselves.

questions.question: What is the value of a digit in the thousands place?
questions.explanation: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

questions.question: What are the first three odd numbers?
questions.explanation: Odd numbers are natural numbers not divisible by 2.

questions.question: What is the smallest even number greater than 10?
questions.explanation: Even numbers are natural numbers divisible by 2.

notes.title: Indian Number System Periods
notes.content: The Indian number system has four periods: Ones (up to hundreds), Thousands (thousands and ten thousands), Lakhs (lakhs and ten lakhs), and Crores (crores and ten crores).

notes.title: Place Value Explanation
notes.content: Place value is determined by multiplying the digit by the value of its position.

notes.title: Even and Odd Numbers Definition
notes.content: Even numbers are natural numbers divisible by 2, while odd numbers are not divisible by 2.

notes.title: Prime Numbers Definition
notes.content: Prime numbers are natural numbers greater than 1 that have no divisors other than 1 and themselves.

notes.title: Place Value in Decimal Numbers
notes.content: Decimal numbers use a point to separate the whole number part from the fractional part, with each digit's place value determined by its position after the point.

facts.fact: In the Indian system, commas are placed after every two digits from the right, except the first comma which comes after three digits.
The number 0 is a digit and also a placeholder in numbers.
In decimal numbers, the place value of each digit decreases by one as you move to the right of the point.
Prime numbers are used extensively in cryptography and coding theory.
The number system has evolved over time, with various civilizations contributing to its development.
